signal_title: Digital Markets, Competition and Consumers Act 2024
risk_rationale: The new UK legislation introduces measures that could require immediate changes to TikTok's e-commerce interfaces to comply with actions against unfair commercial practices. This poses a material compliance gap that needs to be addressed promptly to avoid enforcement actions.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Return policy minimum: 7-day no-questions-asked (14 in EU)
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new regulation may require additional measures beyond the current return policy to address unfair commercial practices, such as displaying warnings or restricting access to certain interfaces.
The regulation may necessitate additional consumer warnings beyond current live commerce disclosures to ensure compliance with the new Act.

remediations.title: Enhance Consumer Protection Measures
remediations.description: Implement additional consumer protection measures on e-commerce interfaces, including warnings for potentially unfair practices and mechanisms to restrict access if necessary.
acceptance_criteria: Warnings are displayed for identified unfair practices
Mechanisms to restrict access to non-compliant interfaces are implemented

business_impact: Failure to comply with the new UK legislation could result in enforcement actions that may disrupt e-commerce operations and damage brand reputation.
recommended_actions: Conduct a detailed review of e-commerce practices to identify potential unfair practices
Develop and implement consumer warning systems on e-commerce interfaces
Establish a compliance monitoring system to ensure ongoing adherence to the new Act
